title: Prepress Technicians and Workers
description: Format and proof text and images submitted by designers and clients into finished pages that can be printed. Includes digital and photo typesetting. May produce printing plates.

tasks.text: Generate prepress proofs in digital or other format to approximate the appearance of the final printed piece.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Proofread and perform quality control of text and images.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Enter, position, and alter text size, using computers, to make up and arrange pages so that printed materials can be produced.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform "preflight" check of required font, graphic, text and image files to ensure completeness prior to delivery to printer.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate and maintain laser plate-making equipment that converts electronic data to plates without the use of film.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter, store, and retrieve information on computer-aided equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain, adjust, and clean equipment, and perform minor repairs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate presses to print proofs of plates, monitoring printing quality to ensure that it is adequate.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Select proper types of plates according to press run lengths.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine finished plates to detect flaws, verify conformity with master plates, and measure dot sizes and centers, using light boxes and microscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine unexposed photographic plates to detect flaws or foreign particles prior to printing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine photographic images for obvious imperfections prior to plate making.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Scale copy for reductions and enlargements, using proportion wheels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze originals to evaluate color density, gradation highlights, middle tones, and shadows, using densitometers and knowledge of light and color.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Set scanners to specific color densities, sizes, screen rulings, and exposure adjustments, using scanner keyboards or computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
